def: Approximately polygonal; somewhat or almost polygonal.
Threefold. "Trinal unity." Milton.In their trinal triplicities on high. Spenser.
An honorary title given to officers of high rank in Turkey, asto governers of provinces, military commanders, etc. The earlier formwas bashaw. [Written also pacha.]

In Mexico and the southwestern United States, a kind ofplastered house or hut, usually made by planting poles or timber inthe ground, filling in between them with screen work or wickerwork,and daubing one or both sides with mud or adobe mortar; also, thismethod of construction.
Suitable to be patented; capable of being patented.
Any substance having a peculiar glassy appearance, and aconchoidal fracture, and usually produced by fusion.
To get more astern or parallel with the vessel's course; todraw aft; -- said of the wind.
The Marxian socialism and programme of reform throughrevolution as advocated by the French political leader Jules BasileGuesde (pron. ged) (1845- ). -- Guesd"ist (#), n. & a.
To tear up by the roots; to eradicate; to uproot.
